sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.70 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called an of dimension$n$over a ring$R$if it is of type$$over$R$and$^i(G;RG) = 0$for$i n$, while$^n(G;RG) = R$as an$RG$-module, where the action on$R$is trivial. (Note that$G$is not required to be finitely presented.) % If$G$is an orientable Poincar\'e duality group of dimension$n$over all fields, is it an orientable Poincar\'e duality group over the integers?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
